# Low Stock Alert System PRP - BasicAgInventory
<!-- Generated by CODEX Enhanced PRP Creation - Complete Workflow Context Integration -->

## Goal

**Feature Goal**: Prevent costly stockouts and emergency purchases by implementing an intelligent low stock monitoring system that tracks inventory levels against user-defined minimums and delivers timely alerts through push notifications and in-app dashboards, reducing emergency purchases by 90%.

**Deliverable**: Swift-based alert system with Apple Push Notification Service integration, background monitoring, local notification fallbacks, and alert management dashboard following iOS notification best practices and MVVM architecture.

**Success Definition**:
- Users can set custom minimum stock levels for any inventory item
- Push notifications delivered within 5 minutes of stock level breach
- Local notification fallback ensures alerts even without internet connectivity
- Alert dashboard provides clear overview of all low stock items with action items

## User Persona

**Target User**: Farm Manager Sarah - Needs proactive inventory alerts to prevent operational disruptions during critical farming periods

**Use Case**: Continuous monitoring of critical supplies (feed, fuel, medications) with immediate alerts when levels approach minimum thresholds, enabling proactive purchasing before stockouts occur

**User Journey**:
1. Set minimum stock levels for critical inventory items
2. Configure notification preferences (immediate, daily summary, etc.)
3. Receive push notifications when items fall below minimums
4. Review low stock dashboard for comprehensive alert overview
5. Mark items as "ordered" to track purchasing status
6. Receive follow-up alerts for items still below minimum after time periods

**Pain Points Addressed**:
- Unexpected stockouts during critical farm operations
- Manual tracking of inventory levels across multiple locations
- Missed purchasing opportunities due to lack of visibility
- Emergency purchases at premium prices

## Why

**Business Value**:
- Prevents 90% of emergency purchases through proactive alerts
- Reduces inventory carrying costs through optimized purchasing timing
- Eliminates operational downtime from stockouts of critical supplies
- Enables bulk purchasing opportunities through advance planning

**Technical Requirements**:
- FR4: Set minimum stock levels with push notifications when breached
- NFR: Push notifications delivered within 30 seconds when connected
- Performance: Background monitoring without impacting app performance
- Privacy: All alert data stored locally with user consent for notifications

**Architecture Decisions**:
- Apple Push Notification Service for remote notifications
- Local notification framework for offline alert capability
- Background app refresh for continuous monitoring
- Core Data triggers for real-time stock level evaluation

**Integration Needs**:
- Apple Push Notification Service for remote alert delivery
- iOS Local Notification framework for offline alerts
- Background App Refresh for continuous monitoring
- Core Data change tracking for real-time evaluation

## What

This implementation creates a comprehensive alert system that monitors inventory levels continuously and delivers timely notifications through multiple channels. The system provides both immediate alerts for critical items and summary notifications for less urgent items, with intelligent scheduling to avoid notification fatigue.

**Core Features**:
- **Minimum Stock Configuration**: User-defined thresholds per inventory item
- **Real-time Monitoring**: Continuous background evaluation of stock levels
- **Multi-channel Alerts**: Push notifications, local notifications, and in-app alerts
- **Alert Dashboard**: Centralized view of all low stock items with status tracking
- **Notification Preferences**: Configurable alert timing and delivery methods
- **Purchase Tracking**: Mark items as "ordered" with follow-up alert scheduling
- **Smart Scheduling**: Intelligent batching to prevent notification spam
- **Offline Capability**: Local notifications when push notifications unavailable

### Success Criteria

- [ ] Minimum stock levels can be set for any inventory item in under 15 seconds
- [ ] Push notifications delivered within 5 minutes of stock level breach (when connected)
- [ ] Local notifications function correctly when device is offline
- [ ] Alert dashboard loads within 1 second showing all low stock items
- [ ] Background monitoring operates without impacting app performance
- [ ] Notification preferences save and apply correctly
- [ ] Purchase tracking updates alert status appropriately
- [ ] Smart scheduling prevents notification fatigue while ensuring critical alerts

### Known Gotchas & Constraints

```swift
// CRITICAL: iOS notification system constraints and limitations

// Push Notification Requirements:
// - Requires Apple Developer Program membership for APNs certificates
// - Device token required for each installation - changes on app reinstall
// - Notification delivery not guaranteed - iOS may throttle based on user behavior
// - 4KB payload limit for notification content

// Background Processing Constraints:
// - Background App Refresh user-controlled setting - may be disabled
// - Limited execution time (30 seconds typical) for background tasks
// - System may terminate background processing if resources needed
// - Background tasks must complete quickly to avoid system termination

import UserNotifications
import BackgroundTasks

// Local Notification Authorization:
// Must request permission before scheduling any notifications
func requestNotificationPermission() async -> Bool {
    let center = UNUserNotificationCenter.current()

    do {
        let granted = try await center.requestAuthorization(options: [.alert, .sound, .badge])
        return granted
    } catch {
        return false
    }
}

// Background Task Registration:
// Must be registered in Info.plist and during app launch
func registerBackgroundTasks() {
    BGTaskScheduler.shared.register(forTaskWithIdentifier: "com.basicaginventory.stockmonitoring",
                                   using: nil) { task in
        self.handleStockMonitoringTask(task as! BGAppRefreshTask)
    }
}

// Core Data Change Monitoring:
// Use NSManagedObjectContext change notifications for real-time monitoring
// Avoid KVO on NSManagedObject - use Core Data notifications instead

// Performance Considerations:
// - Alert evaluation must complete within background time limits
// - Large inventory datasets require efficient querying strategies
// - Notification scheduling should batch updates to prevent system throttling
```

## Implementation Blueprint

### Data Models and Structure

```swift
// Alert configuration and tracking models
// Extends existing Core Data model with notification support

import CoreData
import Foundation
import UserNotifications

// User alert preferences
@objc(AlertPreferences)
public class AlertPreferences: NSManagedObject {
    @NSManaged public var id: UUID
    @NSManaged public var enablePushNotifications: Bool
    @NSManaged public var enableLocalNotifications: Bool
    @NSManaged public var alertTiming: String // "immediate", "daily", "weekly"
    @NSManaged public var quietHoursStart: Date?
    @NSManaged public var quietHoursEnd: Date?
    @NSManaged public var createdAt: Date
    @NSManaged public var updatedAt: Date

    // Default notification preferences for farm operations
    static func defaultPreferences(context: NSManagedObjectContext) -> AlertPreferences {
        let prefs = AlertPreferences(context: context)
        prefs.id = UUID()
        prefs.enablePushNotifications = true
        prefs.enableLocalNotifications = true
        prefs.alertTiming = "immediate"
        prefs.createdAt = Date()
        prefs.updatedAt = Date()
        return prefs
    }
}

// Alert tracking for individual items
@objc(LowStockAlert)
public class LowStockAlert: NSManagedObject {
    @NSManaged public var id: UUID
    @NSManaged public var alertType: String // "low_stock", "critical_stock", "out_of_stock"
    @NSManaged public var severity: String // "low", "medium", "high", "critical"
    @NSManaged public var status: String // "active", "acknowledged", "resolved", "snoozed"
    @NSManaged public var createdAt: Date
    @NSManaged public var acknowledgedAt: Date?
    @NSManaged public var resolvedAt: Date?
    @NSManaged public var snoozeUntil: Date?
    @NSManaged public var lastNotificationSent: Date?
    @NSManaged public var notificationCount: Int32

    // Relationships
    @NSManaged public var inventoryItem: InventoryItem
    @NSManaged public var location: Location?

    // Computed properties for alert management
    var isActive: Bool {
        status == "active" && (snoozeUntil == nil || snoozeUntil! < Date())
    }

    var shouldSendNotification: Bool {
        guard isActive else { return false }

        // Prevent notification spam - minimum 1 hour between notifications
        if let lastSent = lastNotificationSent {
            return Date().timeIntervalSince(lastSent) >= 3600
        }

        return true
    }

    // Alert priority for notification scheduling
    var priority: UNNotificationRequest.Priority {
        switch severity {
        case "critical": return .high
        case "high": return .high
        case "medium": return .normal
        default: return .low
        }
    }
}

// Extend InventoryItem with alert capabilities
extension InventoryItem {
    @NSManaged public var minimumStock: Int32
    @NSManaged public var alertThreshold: Int32 // Warning level before minimum
    @NSManaged public var enableAlerts: Bool
    @NSManaged public var lastStockCheck: Date?

    // Alert status computation
    var stockStatus: StockStatus {
        if quantity <= 0 {
            return .outOfStock
        } else if quantity <= minimumStock {
            return .belowMinimum
        } else if quantity <= alertThreshold {
            return .approaching
        } else {
            return .adequate
        }
    }

    var needsAlert: Bool {
        return enableAlerts && (stockStatus == .belowMinimum || stockStatus == .outOfStock)
    }
}

enum StockStatus: String, CaseIterable {
    case adequate = "adequate"
    case approaching = "approaching"
    case belowMinimum = "below_minimum"
    case outOfStock = "out_of_stock"

    var displayName: String {
        switch self {
        case .adequate: return "Adequate Stock"
        case .approaching: return "Stock Running Low"
        case .belowMinimum: return "Below Minimum"
        case .outOfStock: return "Out of Stock"
        }
    }

    var alertSeverity: String {
        switch self {
        case .adequate: return "none"
        case .approaching: return "low"
        case .belowMinimum: return "high"
        case .outOfStock: return "critical"
        }
    }
}

// Alert business logic errors
enum AlertError: Error, LocalizedError {
    case notificationPermissionDenied
    case backgroundRefreshDisabled
    case invalidThreshold
    case alertCreationFailed

    var errorDescription: String? {
        switch self {
        case .notificationPermissionDenied:
            return "Notification permission required for stock alerts"
        case .backgroundRefreshDisabled:
            return "Background refresh needed for continuous monitoring"
        case .invalidThreshold:
            return "Minimum stock must be greater than zero"
        case .alertCreationFailed:
            return "Failed to create stock alert"
        }
    }
}
```

### Implementation Patterns & Key Details

```swift
// Notification Service Pattern with iOS UserNotifications
import UserNotifications
import Combine

class NotificationService: NSObject, ObservableObject, UNUserNotificationCenterDelegate {
    @Published var authorizationStatus: UNAuthorizationStatus = .notDetermined
    @Published var notificationSettings: UNNotificationSettings?

    private let center = UNUserNotificationCenter.current()

    override init() {
        super.init()
        center.delegate = self
        updateNotificationSettings()
    }

    // Request notification permission for alert system
    func requestAuthorization() async -> Bool {
        do {
            let granted = try await center.requestAuthorization(options: [.alert, .sound, .badge, .provisional])
            await MainActor.run {
                authorizationStatus = granted ? .authorized : .denied
            }
            return granted
        } catch {
            await MainActor.run {
                authorizationStatus = .denied
            }
            return false
        }
    }

    // Schedule local notification for stock alert
    func scheduleStockAlert(for item: InventoryItem, severity: String) async throws {
        let content = UNMutableNotificationContent()
        content.title = "Low Stock Alert"
        content.body = "\(item.name) is running low (\(item.quantity) remaining)"
        content.sound = severity == "critical" ? .defaultCritical : .default
        content.categoryIdentifier = "STOCK_ALERT"

        // Custom data for alert handling
        content.userInfo = [
            "itemId": item.id.uuidString,
            "severity": severity,
            "currentQuantity": item.quantity,
            "minimumStock": item.minimumStock
        ]

        // Immediate delivery for critical alerts, otherwise respect user preferences
        let trigger: UNNotificationTrigger? = severity == "critical" ? nil :
            UNTimeIntervalNotificationTrigger(timeInterval: 1, repeats: false)

        let request = UNNotificationRequest(
            identifier: "stock-alert-\(item.id.uuidString)",
            content: content,
            trigger: trigger
        )

        try await center.add(request)
    }

    // UNUserNotificationCenterDelegate implementation
    func userNotificationCenter(_ center: UNUserNotificationCenter,
                              willPresent notification: UNNotification,
                              withCompletionHandler completionHandler: @escaping (UNNotificationPresentationOptions) -> Void) {
        // Show notification even when app is active
        completionHandler([.banner, .sound, .badge])
    }

    func userNotificationCenter(_ center: UNUserNotificationCenter,
                              didReceive response: UNNotificationResponse,
                              withCompletionHandler completionHandler: @escaping () -> Void) {
        // Handle notification tap - navigate to specific alert
        handleNotificationResponse(response)
        completionHandler()
    }

    private func updateNotificationSettings() {
        center.getNotificationSettings { settings in
            DispatchQueue.main.async {
                self.authorizationStatus = settings.authorizationStatus
                self.notificationSettings = settings
            }
        }
    }
}

// Alert Monitoring Service with Core Data Integration
import CoreData
import BackgroundTasks

class AlertMonitoringService: ObservableObject {
    @Published private(set) var activeAlerts: [LowStockAlert] = []
    @Published private(set) var monitoringEnabled: Bool = true

    private let context: NSManagedObjectContext
    private let notificationService: NotificationService
    private var alertRepository: AlertRepository

    init(context: NSManagedObjectContext, notificationService: NotificationService) {
        self.context = context
        self.notificationService = notificationService
        self.alertRepository = AlertRepository(context: context)

        setupCoreDataMonitoring()
        loadActiveAlerts()
    }

    // Monitor inventory changes for alert conditions
    private func setupCoreDataMonitoring() {
        NotificationCenter.default.addObserver(
            self,
            selector: #selector(contextDidSave(_:)),
            name: .NSManagedObjectContextDidSave,
            object: context
        )
    }

    @objc private func contextDidSave(_ notification: Notification) {
        Task {
            await evaluateStockLevels()
        }
    }

    // Evaluate all inventory items for alert conditions
    private func evaluateStockLevels() async {
        let request: NSFetchRequest<InventoryItem> = InventoryItem.fetchRequest()
        request.predicate = NSPredicate(format: "enableAlerts == true")

        do {
            let items = try await context.perform {
                try self.context.fetch(request)
            }

            for item in items {
                await processItemAlertStatus(item)
            }

            await loadActiveAlerts()

        } catch {
            print("Failed to evaluate stock levels: \(error)")
        }
    }

    // Process individual item for alert creation/updates
    private func processItemAlertStatus(_ item: InventoryItem) async {
        let stockStatus = item.stockStatus

        switch stockStatus {
        case .belowMinimum, .outOfStock:
            await createOrUpdateAlert(for: item, status: stockStatus)
        case .adequate:
            await resolveAlert(for: item)
        case .approaching:
            // Optional: Create warning alert based on user preferences
            break
        }
    }

    private func createOrUpdateAlert(for item: InventoryItem, status: StockStatus) async {
        do {
            // Check for existing active alert
            if let existingAlert = try await alertRepository.findActiveAlert(for: item) {
                existingAlert.severity = status.alertSeverity
                existingAlert.updatedAt = Date()

                // Send notification if threshold met
                if existingAlert.shouldSendNotification {
                    try await notificationService.scheduleStockAlert(for: item, severity: existingAlert.severity)
                    existingAlert.lastNotificationSent = Date()
                    existingAlert.notificationCount += 1
                }
            } else {
                // Create new alert
                let alert = LowStockAlert(context: context)
                alert.id = UUID()
                alert.alertType = status == .outOfStock ? "out_of_stock" : "low_stock"
                alert.severity = status.alertSeverity
                alert.status = "active"
                alert.createdAt = Date()
                alert.inventoryItem = item
                alert.location = item.location
                alert.notificationCount = 0

                // Send immediate notification for new alert
                try await notificationService.scheduleStockAlert(for: item, severity: alert.severity)
                alert.lastNotificationSent = Date()
                alert.notificationCount = 1
            }

            try await context.perform {
                try self.context.save()
            }

        } catch {
            print("Failed to create/update alert for \(item.name): \(error)")
        }
    }
}

// Background Task for Continuous Monitoring
import BackgroundTasks

class StockMonitoringTask {
    static let identifier = "com.basicaginventory.stockmonitoring"

    static func register() {
        BGTaskScheduler.shared.register(forTaskWithIdentifier: identifier,
                                       using: nil) { task in
            handleBackgroundMonitoring(task as! BGAppRefreshTask)
        }
    }

    static func schedule() {
        let request = BGAppRefreshTaskRequest(identifier: identifier)
        request.earliestBeginDate = Date(timeIntervalSinceNow: 15 * 60) // 15 minutes

        do {
            try BGTaskScheduler.shared.submit(request)
        } catch {
            print("Failed to schedule background task: \(error)")
        }
    }

    private static func handleBackgroundMonitoring(_ task: BGAppRefreshTask) {
        // Schedule next background refresh
        schedule()

        task.expirationHandler = {
            task.setTaskCompleted(success: false)
        }

        // Perform efficient stock level check
        Task {
            do {
                // Quick evaluation of critical items only during background
                let alertService = AlertMonitoringService.shared
                await alertService.evaluateStockLevels()

                task.setTaskCompleted(success: true)
            } catch {
                task.setTaskCompleted(success: false)
            }
        }
    }
}

// SwiftUI Alert Dashboard Implementation
@MainActor
class AlertDashboardViewModel: ObservableObject {
    @Published private(set) var alerts: [LowStockAlert] = []
    @Published private(set) var criticalAlerts: [LowStockAlert] = []
    @Published private(set) var isLoading: Bool = false
    @Published var filterLocation: Location?
    @Published var showResolvedAlerts: Bool = false

    private let alertRepository: AlertRepository
    private let alertMonitoringService: AlertMonitoringService

    init(alertRepository: AlertRepository, alertMonitoringService: AlertMonitoringService) {
        self.alertRepository = alertRepository
        self.alertMonitoringService = alertMonitoringService
        loadAlerts()
    }

    func loadAlerts() {
        isLoading = true

        Task {
            do {
                let loadedAlerts = try await alertRepository.fetchAlerts(
                    location: filterLocation,
                    includeResolved: showResolvedAlerts
                )

                await MainActor.run {
                    self.alerts = loadedAlerts
                    self.criticalAlerts = loadedAlerts.filter { $0.severity == "critical" }
                    self.isLoading = false
                }
            } catch {
                await MainActor.run {
                    self.isLoading = false
                }
            }
        }
    }

    func acknowledgeAlert(_ alert: LowStockAlert) async {
        do {
            try await alertRepository.acknowledgeAlert(alert)
            loadAlerts()
        } catch {
            print("Failed to acknowledge alert: \(error)")
        }
    }

    func markAsOrdered(_ alert: LowStockAlert) async {
        do {
            try await alertRepository.markAsOrdered(alert)
            loadAlerts()
        } catch {
            print("Failed to mark as ordered: \(error)")
        }
    }
}
```

### Integration Points

```yaml
INTERNAL_INTEGRATION:
  - component: InventoryManagement
    interface: InventoryItem entity extended with minimum stock and alert fields
    data_flow: Quantity updates trigger alert evaluation through Core Data observers
    error_handling: Alert system gracefully handles inventory operation failures

  - component: LocationManagement
    interface: Location-aware alert filtering and notification context
    data_flow: Location information included in alert notifications for context
    error_handling: Alert system functions with or without location data

EXTERNAL_INTEGRATION:
  - service: Apple Push Notification Service (APNs)
    protocol: HTTP/2 API with JWT authentication for remote notifications
    authentication: APNs certificates and device token management
    fallback: Local notifications when push notifications unavailable

  - service: iOS UserNotifications Framework
    protocol: Native iOS notification APIs with authorization flow
    authentication: User permission required for notification delivery
    fallback: In-app alerts when notification permission denied

  - service: iOS Background Tasks Framework
    protocol: BGAppRefreshTask for background stock monitoring
    authentication: Background execution requires user-enabled background refresh
    fallback: Foreground-only monitoring when background refresh disabled

STATE_MANAGEMENT:
  - pattern: SwiftUI MVVM with alert-aware ObservableObject ViewModels
  - persistence: Core Data alert entities with encrypted local storage
  - synchronization: Real-time UI updates via Core Data change notifications and Combine
```

## Validation Loop

### Level 1: Syntax & Style (Immediate Feedback)

```bash
# iOS notification framework integration validation
swiftlint lint --config .swiftlint.yml --strict
swift-format lint --recursive Sources/

# UserNotifications framework compilation check
swift build -c debug

# Info.plist validation for notification permissions
/usr/libexec/PlistBuddy -c "Print :NSUserNotificationAlertStyle" Info.plist

# Expected: No errors, notification usage descriptions present
```

### Level 2: Unit Tests (Component Validation)

```bash
# Alert system and notification service tests
swift test --filter AlertTests
swift test --filter NotificationServiceTests
swift test --filter AlertMonitoringServiceTests

# Background task registration and execution tests
swift test --filter BackgroundTaskTests

# Mock notification testing for various scenarios
swift test --filter NotificationMockTests

# Expected: All unit tests pass, notification scenarios covered
```

### Level 3: Integration Testing (System Validation)

```bash
# iOS notification system integration testing
xcodebuild test -scheme BasicAgInventory -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 15'

# Push notification integration testing (requires physical device)
xcodebuild test -scheme BasicAgInventory -destination 'platform=iOS,name=[Device Name]'

# Background task execution validation
# Alert delivery timing and accuracy testing

# Integration validation checklist:
# - [ ] Notification permission request functions correctly
# - [ ] Local notifications schedule and deliver appropriately
# - [ ] Background monitoring operates within system limits
# - [ ] Alert dashboard updates reflect real-time changes
```

### Level 4: Acceptance Testing (Requirements Validation)

```bash
# User story acceptance criteria validation
# Epic 3: Low Stock Management validation:

# - [ ] Story 3.1: Set minimum stock levels with alert notifications
# - [ ] Story 3.2: View usage patterns and consumption trends
# - [ ] FR4: Minimum stock levels with push notifications when breached
# - [ ] Performance: Notifications delivered within 5 minutes when connected
# - [ ] Privacy: Local storage of alert preferences with user consent
```

## Final Validation Checklist

### Requirements Traceability

- [ ] Background monitoring without performance impact - ✅ Efficient background processing
- [ ] Local notification fallback capability - ✅ Offline alert functionality
- [ ] Alert dashboard with action items - ✅ Comprehensive alert management UI
- [ ] All user stories from Epic 3 have working implementations

### Architecture Compliance
- [ ] Follows iOS notification best practices with proper permission handling
- [ ] Uses Apple Push Notification Service and UserNotifications frameworks correctly
- [ ] Implements background processing within iOS system limitations
- [ ] Maintains offline-first capability with local notification fallbacks
- [ ] Uses encrypted Core Data storage for alert preferences and history

### Code Quality
- [ ] Follows iOS notification development guidelines and conventions
- [ ] Implements comprehensive error handling for notification delivery failures
- [ ] Includes accessibility features for alert management interface
- [ ] Uses efficient Core Data queries for background monitoring

### Testing & Validation
- [ ] Unit tests cover notification service and alert monitoring logic
- [ ] Integration tests validate iOS notification system functionality
- [ ] Performance tests verify background monitoring efficiency
- [ ] User acceptance tests confirm alert delivery and management workflows

### Documentation & Deployment
- [ ] iOS notification usage descriptions in Info.plist for App Store approval
- [ ] APNs certificate setup documentation for production deployment
- [ ] Background task entitlements configured for continuous monitoring
- [ ] Alert privacy policy compliance for agricultural data handling

---

## Anti-Patterns to Avoid

- ❌ Don't schedule notifications without user permission - always check authorization status
- ❌ Don't ignore iOS notification delivery limitations - implement local fallbacks
- ❌ Don't perform heavy processing in background tasks - keep operations lightweight
- ❌ Don't spam users with notifications - implement intelligent scheduling and batching
- ❌ Don't assume push notifications will be delivered - provide in-app alternatives
- ❌ Don't store sensitive alert data in notification payloads - use identifiers only
- ❌ Don't ignore background app refresh user settings - gracefully handle disabled state
- ❌ Don't implement alert logic without proper error handling and recovery mechanisms

## Confidence Score: 9/10

High confidence based on comprehensive iOS notification system implementation patterns, proven background processing approaches, and complete context synthesis from architecture and requirements.

- [x] Fresh Claude instance could implement with only this PRP and referenced iOS documentation

- [x] No implicit iOS development knowledge required beyond documented APIs
- [x] Implementation path is clear with ordered dependencies and validation steps
